original_abstract: This study presents a systematic literature review (SLR) exploring the research trends of STEM and STEAM education within the Indonesian context from 2016 to 2021. Utilizing the PRISMA framework and the Paper Classification Form (PCF), 63 peer-reviewed articles from national and international databases were analyzed. The findings reveal a significant upward trend in publication volume over the last six years, with the majority of studies employing experimental research methods. Learning strategies emerged as the most investigated topic, primarily focused on science subjects at the secondary school level. Results indicate that project-based learning (PjBL), problem-based learning (PBL), and inquiry-based models are the most prevalent frameworks used to integrate STEM/STEAM into the classroom. Furthermore, the analysis identifies critical thinking, scientific literacy, and general learning outcomes as the primary themes associated with this educational approach. The review concludes that while STEM education is maturing in Indonesia, there is a substantial need for more holistic empirical research into STEAM, specifically regarding the role of the arts and the use of advanced educational technologies like robotics and gamification to foster 21st-century skills.
humanized_abstract: This study conducted a systematic literature review (SLR) aimed at analyzing the trends of research on STEM and STEAM education in Indonesia in the period 2016–2021. According to the PRISMA (Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analyses) principles and the Paper Classification Form (PCF) format, 63 papers obtained from domestic and foreign publications were analyzed. The results show that the publications in Indonesia on STEM and STEAM are increasing significantly within the last six years, with the majority employing the experimental method. The largest number of papers dealt with learning strategies, particularly focusing on science subjects in secondary school students. It is found that the frameworks employed in project-based learning (PjBL), problem-based learning (PBL), and inquiry-based learning (IBL) for integrating STEM/STEAM in the classroom are the most dominant. The dominant skills acquired by students from STEM/STEAM learning activities are those of critical thinking, scientific literacy, and general learning skills. Therefore, STEM education has been developed maturely in Indonesia. It is urgently needed to conduct more comprehensive and empirical research related to STEAM education in Indonesia in order to analyze and answer the role of the arts and the role of technology in developing 21st-century skills.
